wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the OSCAR project overview section focused on the AI-powered voice-to-text application. Explain its core purpose as a speech-to-text solution that converts spoken words into clean, formatted text using AI. Document the key value propositions, target audience, and primary use cases. Include the technology stack overview covering Next.js 15, Supabase, DeepSeek AI integration, and payment processing. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining voice recording workflows and AI text formatting, while including technical details for experienced developers about the architecture and implementation patterns. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples demonstrating the complete user journey from voice input to formatted output.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for OSCAR focused on rapid onboarding and initial setup. Document prerequisites including Node.js 18+, the required stt-tts-lib package, and system requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including dependency installation, environment setup, and running the development server. Explain the audio-to-text library integration and environment variable configuration. Include practical examples of first-time usage, from starting the development server to completing the first voice recording and text formatting. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners. Make content accessible to developers new to the project while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for OSCAR's system design focused on the full-stack architecture combining frontend and backend components. Describe the high-level design using Next.js App Router with server components, Supabase integration for authentication and database, and external AI services for text processing. Document component interactions between UI components, service layers, and external APIs. Explain technical decisions including the choice of Supabase for authentication and data storage, Next.js server components for performance, and modular service architecture. Include infrastructure requirements, session management through middleware, and data flow patterns. Provide system context diagrams showing the relationship between browser clients, server-side API routes, Supabase database, and external AI services. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication security, rate limiting, and real-time data synchronization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Recording System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the voice recording system focused on the browser-based audio capture and processing implementation. Thoroughly explain the MediaRecorder API integration, audio format handling (WebM), and real-time recording controls. Document the implementation details of the recording button component, timer functionality, and permission management. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing audio capture workflow, session storage management, and error handling for microphone permissions. Document configuration options for recording quality, duration limits, and browser compatibility. Explain relationships with the audio-to-text processing service and state management. Address common issues like permission errors, browser limitations, and audio quality optimization. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers implementing similar functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Text Processing Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI text processing engine focused on DeepSeek API integration and text formatting capabilities. Document the main AI service implementation, fallback mechanisms using local formatting, and prompt engineering strategies. Explain the text formatting algorithms, title generation, and translation services. Include detailed API documentation for all DeepSeek endpoints including format, title, and translate operations. Document request/response schemas, authentication requirements, and error handling. Provide concrete examples of AI processing workflows, custom vocabulary integration, and prompt optimization techniques. Address security considerations for AI prompts, rate limiting, and cost optimization strategies. Include troubleshooting guidance for API failures and performance tuning recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the content management system focused on note creation, editing, and organization features. Thoroughly explain the note editor implementation, real-time editing capabilities, and export functionality. Document the note actions system including copy, download, star/unstar, and deletion operations. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing note data structures, state management, and UI interactions. Document the skeleton loading states, user feedback mechanisms, and data persistence strategies. Explain relationships with the AI processing system and user authentication. Address common issues like data synchronization, offline storage, and performance optimization for large notes. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers working with the note management APIs.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management & Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user management and authentication system focused on Supabase integration and session management. Document the client-side and server-side Supabase configurations, authentication flows, and session handling. Explain the middleware implementation for automatic session refresh and authentication protection. Include detailed examples of user registration, login, password reset, and account management workflows. Document the authentication edge button implementation and social login integration. Address security considerations including JWT token handling, row-level security policies, and session expiration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common authentication issues and performance optimization for user sessions. Include migration guides for authentication changes and best practices for user data protection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subscription & Payment System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the subscription and payment system focused on Razorpay integration and usage-based billing. Thoroughly explain the subscription management implementation, pricing tier configuration, and payment processing workflows. Document the usage tracking system, rate limiting, and consumption monitoring. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing subscription creation, renewal handling, and webhook processing. Document the pricing card components, checkout flow, and usage indicator implementation. Explain relationships with the authentication system and user limits. Address common issues like payment failures, subscription cancellations, and usage quota enforcement. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers implementing similar payment systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema & Migrations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for OSCAR's database schema focused on Supabase integration and table relationships. Detail the vocabulary, feedback, and subscription table structures, field definitions, and data types. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints including row-level security policies. Explain data validation rules, business rules, and data lifecycle management. Include database schema diagrams and sample data for each table. Document data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Specify data retention policies, archival rules, and migration paths. Address data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms. Include migration management procedures and version compatibility considerations for database schema evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for OSCAR's REST endpoints focused on all public interfaces and internal API routes. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Include comprehensive examples for text formatting, title generation, translation, email formatting, subscription creation, webhook handling, and usage tracking APIs. Document error handling strategies, status codes, and rate limiting policies. Provide client implementation guidelines with code examples in multiple languages. Address security considerations including API key management, request validation, and protection against abuse. Include performance optimization tips, caching strategies, and monitoring approaches. Document common use cases, integration patterns, and troubleshooting guidance for API consumers.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Component Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for OSCAR's UI component library focused on reusable React components and design system. Describe the component catalog including buttons, inputs, cards, selects, and textareas built with Radix UI and Tailwind CSS. Document the floating navbar, footer, and settings components. Include usage examples with code snippets showing component props, events, and customization options. Provide guidelines for responsive design, accessibility compliance, and theme consistency. Document component states, animations, and transitions. Include style customization options, theming support, and component composition patterns. Address cross-browser compatibility, performance optimization, and integration with other UI elements. Explain the design system principles and component architecture patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for OSCAR's configuration and customization options focused on build settings, environment configuration, and application customization. Document Next.js configuration including build optimization, image optimization, and runtime configuration. Explain TypeScript configuration, Tailwind CSS setup, and component library configuration. Include environment variable management, constant definitions, and utility function usage. Provide examples of customizing the application appearance, behavior, and functionality. Address performance optimization settings, bundle analysis, and deployment configuration. Document extension points for adding new features, integrating additional services, and modifying existing functionality. Include best practices for maintaining configuration across development, staging, and production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
I'll analyze the OSCAR project structure and generate a comprehensive overview. Let me start by examining the key components and their relationships.

<blog>

# OSCAR - AI Voice Notes: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
OSCAR is an AI-powered voice-to-text application that transforms spoken words into clean, formatted text with minimal user effort. The platform enables users to capture ideas, meetings, and thoughts through natural speech while leveraging artificial intelligence to format and structure the content automatically.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Voice-to-Text Conversion**: Provide seamless speech-to-text functionality using browser-based technologies
- **Intelligent Formatting**: Apply AI-driven text formatting to remove filler words, correct grammar, and structure content
- **User Experience**: Deliver a frictionless experience where users simply speak while the app handles the rest
- **Accessibility**: Support multiple languages and provide customizable vocabulary for specialized terminology

### Target Audience
- Professionals seeking efficient meeting documentation
- Students capturing lectures and study materials
- Content creators and writers needing quick note-taking solutions
- Anyone who thinks faster than they can type

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[Layout System]
    A --> C[API Routes]
    A --> D[Components]
    
    B --> E[Root Layout]
    B --> F[Auth Provider]
    B --> G[Subscription Provider]
    
    C --> H[DeepSeek AI Services]
    C --> I[Razorpay Payments]
    C --> J[Usage Tracking]
    
    D --> K[Recording Components]
    D --> L[UI Components]
    D --> M[Settings Components]
    
    H --> N[Text Formatting]
    H --> O[Title Generation]
    H --> P[Translation Services]
```

### Design Patterns
The project follows modern React patterns with:
- **Server Components**: Leveraging Next.js 15's server component capabilities
- **Context Pattern**: Managing authentication and subscription state globally
- **Provider Pattern**: Encapsulating state management for auth and subscriptions
- **Component Composition**: Building complex UI from smaller, reusable components

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant R as Recording Button
    participant S as Speech Recognition
    participant A as AI Processing
    participant F as Formatting Service
    
    U->>R: Click Start Recording
    R->>S: Initialize MediaRecorder
    S->>U: Capture Audio Stream
    U->>S: Stop Recording
    S->>F: Send Raw Audio
    F->>A: Process with AI
    A->>F: Formatted Text Response
    F->>U: Display Results
```

### Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[MediaRecorder API]
    B --> C[Audio Buffer]
    C --> D[Speech-to-Text Engine]
    D --> E[Raw Transcript]
    E --> F[AI Formatter]
    F --> G[Custom Vocabulary]
    G --> H[Enhanced Formatting]
    H --> I[Final Formatted Text]
    I --> J[Storage Service]
    J --> K[Results Display]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Root**: `app/layout.tsx` - Central layout with providers and global styling
- **Homepage**: `app/page.tsx` - Marketing-focused landing page with feature showcase
- **Recording Interface**: `components/recording/HomeRecordingButton.tsx` - Primary interaction point

### Core Modules
- **Storage Service**: `lib/services/storage.service.ts` - Session-based data persistence
- **Constants & Configuration**: `lib/constants.ts` - Centralized configuration management
- **AI Prompts**: `lib/prompts.ts` - Security-hardened prompt engineering system
- **Rate Limiting**: `lib/middleware/rate-limit.ts` - In-memory rate limiting solution

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: `.env.local` for API keys and configuration
- **Middleware Configuration**: `middleware.ts` for session management and route protection
- **Build Configuration**: `next.config.js`, `tsconfig.json` for TypeScript setup

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **Next.js 15**: Modern React framework with server components
- **Supabase**: Authentication and database services
- **Radix UI**: Accessible UI component library
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first styling framework
- **ONNX Runtime**: Machine learning inference engine

### Integration Points
- **DeepSeek API**: AI-powered text formatting and generation
- **Razorpay**: Payment processing for subscription management
- **Supabase Auth**: User authentication and session management
- **Browser APIs**: MediaRecorder for audio capture

### Component Relationship Architecture

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Auth Context] --> B[Subscription Context]
    B --> C[Recording Components]
    C --> D[Results Components]
    D --> E[Settings Components]
    
    F[UI Components] --> G[Form Elements]
    F --> H[Navigation]
    F --> I[Feedback Widgets]
    
    J[Storage Service] --> K[Session Storage]
    J --> L[Local Storage]
    
    M[API Services] --> N[DeepSeek Integration]
    M --> O[Payment Processing]
    M --> P[Usage Tracking]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Voice Recording & Processing
- **Real-time Audio Capture**: Utilizes browser MediaRecorder API for high-quality audio recording
- **Multi-format Support**: Handles various audio formats with automatic conversion
- **Continuous Recording**: Supports extended recording sessions with auto-save intervals

#### AI-Powered Text Processing
- **Intelligent Formatting**: Removes filler words, corrects grammar, and structures content
- **Custom Vocabulary**: Learns and adapts to user-specific terminology and jargon
- **Multi-language Support**: Handles English and Hindi content with context awareness

#### User Experience Features
- **Progressive Web App**: Works offline and can be installed on mobile devices
- **Responsive Design**: Optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile experiences
- **Accessibility**: Screen reader support and keyboard navigation

### Implementation Highlights

#### Security-Focused AI Integration
- **Prompt Injection Prevention**: Comprehensive input sanitization and validation
- **Role-Based System Prompts**: Strict separation between user input and system instructions
- **XML Delimiter Protection**: Explicit encapsulation of user content in AI requests

#### Subscription Management
- **Tiered Pricing**: Free tier with limited recordings, Pro plan with unlimited access
- **Usage Tracking**: Real-time monitoring of API consumption and feature limits
- **Flexible Billing**: Monthly and yearly payment options with currency switching

### Feature Architecture Flow

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Recording: Start Recording
    Recording --> Processing: Stop Recording
    Processing --> Formatted: AI Processing Complete
    Formatted --> Saved: User Saves/Downloads
    Saved --> [*]
    
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Register
    Authenticated --> Recording: Access Granted
    Authenticated --> Settings: Manage Account
    Settings --> Authenticated: Back to App
```

### Advanced Features

#### Intelligent Content Processing
- **Context-Aware Formatting**: Adapts formatting style based on content type and context
- **Grammar Correction**: Advanced language processing with domain-specific terminology
- **Content Structuring**: Automatic paragraph breaks and logical content organization

#### Personalization & Adaptation
- **Learning System**: AI improves accuracy based on user feedback and preferences
- **Custom Terminology**: Dynamic vocabulary building for specialized industries
- **Style Preferences**: Adaptable formatting styles for different content types

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
OSCAR demonstrates a well-architected full-stack application that successfully combines modern frontend frameworks with robust backend services. The project showcases excellent separation of concerns through its modular component structure and comprehensive service layer.

### Key Architectural Strengths
- **Security-First Design**: Comprehensive input validation and prompt injection prevention
- **Scalable Architecture**: Modular design allows for easy feature expansion
- **Performance Optimization**: Efficient use of browser APIs and caching strategies
- **Developer Experience**: Clean code organization with TypeScript and consistent patterns

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **State Persistence**: Consider implementing server-side state management for better reliability
- **Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and integration test coverage for critical components
- **Monitoring**: Implement comprehensive logging and error tracking for production monitoring
- **Performance Metrics**: Add performance monitoring for AI processing and user experience metrics

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Server-Side State**: Move critical state management to Supabase for better reliability
2. **Expand Testing Suite**: Add comprehensive Jest tests for all service layers and components
3. **Add Monitoring**: Integrate Sentry or similar for error tracking and performance monitoring
4. **Optimize AI Costs**: Implement caching strategies for frequently processed content types
5. **Enhance Analytics**: Add usage analytics to understand user behavior and optimize features

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile App Development**: Consider React Native implementation for native mobile experiences
- **Advanced AI Features**: Explore multimodal processing and real-time transcription capabilities
- **Team Collaboration**: Add collaborative features for shared note editing and commenting
- **Export Formats**: Expand export capabilities to include PDF, DOCX, and other formats

The OSCAR project represents a mature, production-ready application that effectively solves a real-world problem through innovative technology integration. Its clean architecture and focus on user experience position it well for continued growth and feature expansion.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [page.tsx](app/page.tsx)
- [utils.ts](lib/utils.ts)
- [prompts.ts](lib/prompts.ts)
- [HomeRecordingButton.tsx](components/recording/HomeRecordingButton.tsx)
- [storage.service.ts](lib/services/storage.service.ts)
- [constants.ts](lib/constants.ts)
- [middleware.ts](middleware.ts)
- [middleware.ts](lib/supabase/middleware.ts)
- [route.ts](app/api/deepseek/format/route.ts)
- [rate-limit.ts](lib/middleware/rate-limit.ts)

</blog>
